rulings.Case Title: ROBERT DORNER VS. RACHAEL ROBBINS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Discovery From Plaintiff-Cross-Defendant Robert Dorner, Individually And Dba Extra Care Contractor And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, December 11, 2019, Line 1, DEFENDANTs IAN MCFARLAND and RACHEL ROBBINS' Motion To Compel Discovery From Plaintiff-Cross-Defendant Robert Dorner, Individually And DBA Extra Care Contractor And Request For Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Tom Cohen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: If he has not already done so, responding party shall serve code compliant responses without objections to all outstanding discovery requests within 10 days. Hearing required as to the responses to Requests for Admissions. The opposition papers show proof of service of verified responses, but do not show the responses themselves. The reply brief does not discuss those responses, and it is not clear if they were actually served. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to tom@thomascohen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ALAN CLAIR VS. THOMAS MILLER III ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Defendant Thomas Miller, Iiis Responses To Discovery
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, December 11, 2019, Line 2, PLAINTIFF ALAN CLAIR's Motion To Compel Defendant Thomas Miller, III's Responses To Discovery Pro Tem Judge Tom Cohen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The Motion is granted. Defendant Miller shall, within 10 days, serve code compliant written responses, without objection, to Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories, and Request for Production of Documents. Further, the Truth of the Matters requested are deemed admitted. Sanctions are awarded against Defendant Miller in the amount of $1,110. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to tom@thomascohen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: VINITA BUSSE VS. ANDREW G. PRICE, INDIVIDUALLY, AND D/B/A ARTISANAL ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Motion To Compel Further Responses To (1) Plaintiffs Amended Response To Defendants Form Interrogatories-Employment Law, Set One; (2) Plaintiffs Amended Responses To Defendants Form Interrogatories-General, Set One (3) Plaintiffs Amended Response To Defendants Request For Production Of Documents, Set One; (4) Plaintiffs Amended Response To Defendants Form Interrogatories-General, Set Two; And (5) For Appointment Of Discovery Referee
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, December 11, 2019, Line 6, DEFENDANT ANDREW G. PRICE, INDIVIDUALLY, AND D/B/A ARTISANAL, ARTISANAL TALENT GROUP, INC's Hearing On Motion To Compel Further Responses To (1) Plaintiffs Amended Response To Defendants Form Interrogatories-Employment Law, Set One; (2) Plaintiffs Amended Responses To Defendants Form Interrogatories-General, Set One (3) Plaintiffs Amended Response To Defendants Request For Production Of Documents, Set One; (4) Plaintiffs Amended Response To Defendants Form Interrogatories-General, Set Two; And (5) For Appointment Of Discovery Referee. Defendants Andrew Price and Artisanal Talent Group's motion to compel further responses to three sets of form interrogatories and one set of document requests and for appointment of discovery referee is granted in part. The court adopts in full the recommendations and reasons for those recommendations in the report of Judge Pro Tem James Fleming dated November 28, 2019, Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and ataches the report of Mr. Fleming and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: TODD J. FERRARIS VS. UNITED PARCEL SERVICE, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Site Inspection Of Defendants Facility
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, December 11, 2019, Line 4, PLAINTIFF TODD FERRARIS's Motion To Compel Site Inspection Of Defendants Facility. Pro Tem Judge Tom Cohen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The Motion is granted. Defendant shall allow inspection of the work space at issue within the next 30 days. If the parties cannot agree on a stipulated protective order, the court will consider a protective order to limit the disclosure of trade secrets to designated individuals not to exclude the parties, their attorneys and experts. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to tom@thomascohen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: BENJAMIN RUBIN VS. CHRISTINA LEUNG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Quash Defendants Deposition Subpoenas Or In The Alternative To Modify And Or Limit Deposition Subpoenas; Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, December 11, 2019, Line 5, PLAINTIFF BENJAMIN RUBIN's Motion To Quash Defendants Deposition Subpoenas Or In The Alternative To Modify And Or Limit Deposition Subpoenas; Request For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Tom Cohen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The Motion is granted. The current subpoenas shall be modified in scope as follows: I) medical and billing records relating to only plaintiffs body parts that were injured in the subject incident, namely, the neck, left shoulder, and left thumb; and 2) medical billing, payments, liens, and insurance information as relates to only plaintiffs body parts, namely his neck, left shoulder and left thumb. The subpoenas shall be limited as to time: I) Medical records: from November 24, 2012 to present; 2) Billing Records limited to the dates of service on and after the November 24, 2017 collision date. Sanctions are awarded against defendant in the amount of $2,310. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to tom@thomascohen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)
